input.linkedin_profile_data: Marcus Holt | VP Revenue Operations at Velosio (400 employees, B2B SaaS, logistics workflow automation). 11 years in ops, previously Sr. Director RevOps at CloudRoute. Active LinkedIn poster — published a post 4 days ago about painful lessons from their HubSpot-to-Salesforce migration: data model mismatches, broken attribution, and 6 weeks of stale pipeline data during cutover.
input.company_research: Velosio closed a $22M Series B in November. Currently hiring 3 RevOps analysts and a Salesforce admin. Recent blog post mentions plans to unify their PLG motion with the enterprise sales team by Q3.
input.value_prop: Revenue intelligence platform that maintains accurate pipeline data during and after CRM migrations by syncing activity data in real time — used by RevOps teams who need clean forecasting immediately post-migration.

output.connection_request: Marcus — your post on the HubSpot → Salesforce migration hit close to home. The stale pipeline problem during cutover is exactly what we help RevOps teams solve. Would love to connect.
output.inmail_subject: Pipeline accuracy during your Salesforce migration
output.inmail_body:
Marcus,

Your post earlier this week about the 6 weeks of stale pipeline data during your CRM migration was unusually candid — most RevOps leaders don't talk about that publicly. The attribution breakage and data model mismatches you described are the exact failure modes we see in almost every major CRM transition.

I work with RevOps teams at companies like yours who've just completed or are mid-migration and need clean, trustworthy pipeline data before the dust settles. The typical problem: your new CRM has the structure but not the history, so forecast confidence collapses right when leadership is scrutinizing the numbers hardest.

We built Clari specifically to sit above the CRM layer and maintain activity-to-pipeline linkage in real time — so even when the underlying data model changes, the signal doesn't disappear. Teams that use it during migrations typically recover forecast accuracy 3-4 weeks faster than those relying on the CRM alone.

Given you're three months post-cutover and now scaling toward unifying your PLG and enterprise motions, this might be a useful conversation. Would a 15-minute call this week or next be worth it?

Priya Anand
Clari
